prerequisites.note: The web app (apps/web) is a stub — no React runtime exists until E6 (Web Frontend Shell). This test plan specifies E2E tests to execute once the frontend is built. E4 is infrastructure: i18n translation keys (E4.1), backend error message translation (E4.2), and number/date/currency formatting (E4.3). Tests verify these infrastructure capabilities through UI interactions on pages created by E6+. Tests assume a running API at :3000, a Vite dev server at :5173, and a seeded database.

admin.description: Primary admin user with full access — used for most journeys

limited_user.description: View-only user — used for permission-denied tests

acceptance_criteria_coverage.E4.1-AC5: Build-time ESLint rule — not testable via E2E. Covered by CI lint step.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.E4.2-AC3: System messages stored with translation keys — verified by backend unit tests and audit log inspection, not directly visible in UI.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.E4.2-AC4: Backend message formatting utility — verified by unit tests in packages/i18n, not directly visible in UI.
acceptance_criteria_coverage.E4.3-AC5: Shared package consistency — architectural concern verified by unit tests in packages/shared and packages/i18n.

journeys.name: Login Page Shows Translated English Text
journeys.description: Verify that the login page renders all user-facing strings from the i18n translation system, not hardcoded text or raw translation keys. Every visible label, button, and placeholder must come from the t() helper resolving English locale files.

steps.visual_check: Login form visible with email input field, password input field, and a Sign In / Log In button. All text is in English — no raw translation keys like 'common.submit' or 'auth.login' visible anywhere.

steps.expected_result: Email field has a translated label (e.g., 'Email' or 'Email Address'), not a raw key like 'field.email'
Password field has a translated label (e.g., 'Password'), not a raw key
Button text is a translated English string (e.g., 'Sign In', 'Log In', or 'Submit'), not 'common.submit' or similar raw key
Page heading shows translated text (e.g., 'Sign In to Nexa ERP'), no i18next key fragments visible

journeys.name: Invalid Login Shows Translated Error Message
journeys.description: Submit invalid credentials and verify the error message displayed comes from the errors.json locale file (errors:AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS), not a hardcoded string or raw translation key. The frontend should resolve the messageKey from the API error envelope.

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated with invalid credentials
Login attempt fails, error message displayed
steps.visual_check: Error message visible on the login form: 'Invalid email or password'. This text comes from errors.json key AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS. The raw key 'errors:AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS' must NOT be visible.

steps.expected_result: Error text matches the English translation from errors.json AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS exactly
Raw translation key 'errors:AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS' does NOT appear anywhere on the page — the frontend resolved it via t()

journeys.name: Login Validation Errors Use Interpolated Field Names
journeys.description: Submit the login form with empty fields and verify that validation error messages use the i18next interpolation system. The validation.json template 'required' is '{{field}} is required' — the frontend must resolve this with the actual field name substituted, not show raw '{{field}}' template syntax.

steps.expected_result: Form submits with empty fields, validation errors appear
steps.visual_check: Inline validation errors visible next to email and password fields. Error messages contain resolved field names (e.g., 'email is required'), NOT raw template syntax like '{{field}} is required' or raw keys like 'validation:required'.

steps.expected_result: Email validation error does NOT contain '{{field}}' — i18next interpolation has resolved the variable
No raw namespace prefix 'validation:' visible in the error message
Password validation error has interpolated field name, not template syntax

journeys.name: Account Locked After Repeated Failures Shows Translated Error
journeys.description: Trigger the account lockout by submitting invalid credentials multiple times (6+ attempts). Verify the lockout error message comes from errors.json ACCOUNT_LOCKED key: 'Account temporarily locked due to too many failed attempts'.

steps.expected_result: Form populated with valid email but wrong password
Login fails with 'Invalid email or password'

steps.visual_check: Error message: 'Account temporarily locked due to too many failed attempts' from errors.json ACCOUNT_LOCKED. No raw key visible.

input_data.expected_text: Account temporarily locked due to too many failed attempts

steps.expected_result: Lockout error matches errors.json ACCOUNT_LOCKED translation exactly

journeys.name: Sidebar Navigation Shows Translated Module Names
journeys.description: After login, verify the app shell sidebar navigation renders module names from navigation.json via t('navigation.xxx'), not hardcoded strings or raw keys. Check that Dashboard, System, Users, Settings all show their English translations.

steps.visual_check: App shell loaded with sidebar navigation. Module names visible in sidebar: 'Dashboard', 'System', 'Settings'. These come from navigation.json translations. No raw keys like 'navigation.dashboard' or 'navigation:system' visible.

steps.expected_result: No raw i18n namespace prefix 'navigation:' appears anywhere on the rendered page

journeys.name: User List Page Uses Translated Headers and Action Buttons
journeys.description: Navigate to the user list page and verify all UI elements (column headers, action buttons, status labels, empty states) use translated strings from common.json and navigation.json. Buttons like Create, Edit, Delete, Search, Filter must match their locale file values.

steps.visual_check: User list page with translated UI: page title 'Users' (from navigation.json), action buttons using common.json labels ('Create', 'Search', 'Filter'), column headers in English. No raw translation keys visible.

steps.expected_result: Create button visible with text 'Create' (from common.json)
Search element visible with translated label/placeholder 'Search' (from common.json)
User status labels show 'Active' or 'Inactive' (from common.json), not raw enum values like 'true'/'false'
No raw i18n namespace prefix 'common:' appears anywhere on the page

journeys.name: User Creation Validation Shows Interpolated Field Names
journeys.description: Open the Create User form and submit it empty to trigger Zod validation. Verify the field-level validation errors use i18next interpolation: the validation.json template '{{field}} is required' must render with actual field names like 'email is required', not raw template syntax.

steps.expected_result: Create User form opens (modal or page)
steps.visual_check: Create User form visible with empty fields for email, firstName, lastName, password, role. All field labels are translated English text.

steps.expected_result: Form validation fails, field-level errors appear
steps.visual_check: Validation errors visible next to required fields. Errors show interpolated field names (e.g., 'email is required', 'firstName is required') not raw '{{field}} is required' or 'validation:required'. The top-level error may show 'Please correct the errors below' from errors.json VALIDATION_ERROR.

steps.expected_result: No raw interpolation template '{{field}}' visible — all variables resolved
No raw namespace prefix 'validation:' visible in error messages
Banner text matches errors.json VALIDATION_ERROR key

journeys.name: Duplicate Email Error Uses Translated Message
journeys.description: Create a user with an email that already exists in the system. Verify the error message displayed is 'A user with this email already exists' from errors.json DUPLICATE_EMAIL key, resolved via the messageKey in the API error envelope.

steps.expected_result: Form populated with existing email address
Server returns DUPLICATE_EMAIL error, frontend displays translated message
steps.visual_check: Error message visible: 'A user with this email already exists'. This comes from errors.json DUPLICATE_EMAIL key via the API messageKey. No raw key 'errors:DUPLICATE_EMAIL' visible.

input_data.expected_text: A user with this email already exists

steps.expected_result: Error text matches errors.json DUPLICATE_EMAIL translation exactly

journeys.name: User Locale Preference Change and Fallback Chain
journeys.description: Edit the current user's locale preference and verify the i18n fallback chain (E4.1 AC3): user locale -> company locale -> 'en'. Since MVP only supports 'en', changing the locale should still resolve to English. Verify the locale field exists and is editable on the user profile.

steps.target: Admin user row or edit link
steps.expected_result: User detail/edit page opens for admin user
steps.visual_check: User detail page showing admin user. Locale field visible with current value 'en'. The field is editable (dropdown or text input).

steps.expected_result: Locale field exists on the user edit form, showing the current locale value
User saved successfully, success toast appears
steps.visual_check: Success feedback visible (toast or inline message). The UI still renders in English — since 'en-GB' falls back to 'en' in the i18n fallback chain. All text remains translated correctly.

steps.expected_result: After locale change, sidebar still shows 'Dashboard' in English — fallback chain resolved correctly

journeys.name: Company Profile Default Language for Fallback Chain
journeys.description: Navigate to the company profile settings and verify the defaultLanguage field exists. This field feeds the i18n fallback chain: when a user has no locale set, the company's default language is used before falling back to 'en'.

steps.visual_check: Company profile page visible with company details. A 'Default Language' or 'Language' field is visible showing the company's default locale setting. All labels on this page are translated English text.

steps.expected_result: defaultLanguage field exists on the company profile form, displaying the current company locale (e.g., 'en')
No raw i18n keys visible on the company profile page

journeys.name: Dates Display in DD/MM/YYYY Format for en-GB
journeys.description: Navigate to a page that displays dates (user list with lastLoginAt/createdAt, or dashboard) and verify dates are formatted using the Intl API with en-GB locale: DD/MM/YYYY for short format, not MM/DD/YYYY (US) or ISO 8601 (2026-02-17).

steps.expected_result: User list page loads showing users with date columns
steps.visual_check: User list with date columns (Created At, Last Login). Dates are formatted as DD/MM/YYYY (e.g., '22/02/2026'), NOT as MM/DD/YYYY, ISO 8601 strings, or raw timestamps. This confirms the formatDate() utility with en-GB locale is being used via the useFormatDate() hook.

steps.expected_result: At least one date value matches DD/MM/YYYY pattern (two-digit day, two-digit month, four-digit year separated by forward slashes)
No raw ISO 8601 timestamp fragments visible in date display

journeys.name: Currency Values Display with Correct GBP Formatting
journeys.description: Navigate to a page displaying monetary values (dashboard, company profile with financial settings, or any page showing amounts) and verify GBP currency formatting: £ symbol, comma thousands separator, 2 decimal places. Uses formatCurrency() from @nexa/shared with en-GB locale.

steps.visual_check: Dashboard or main page loaded. If monetary values are displayed (totals, balances, amounts), they should use GBP formatting: £ symbol, comma as thousands separator (e.g., '£1,234.56'), 2 decimal places for GBP (minorUnit=2). If no monetary values exist on dashboard yet, this step is informational.

steps.expected_result: Company profile page loads — may show base currency code
steps.visual_check: Company profile showing base currency setting (GBP). If any monetary amounts are displayed on this page, they use £ symbol and correct decimal formatting.

steps.expected_result: Company's base currency (GBP) is shown — this drives the currency formatting system via Currency.minorUnit

steps.target: Any monetary value on page (if present)
steps.expected_result: Monetary values match GBP pattern: £ followed by digits with comma separators and exactly 2 decimal places

journeys.name: Permission Denied Shows Translated FORBIDDEN Error
journeys.description: Login as a limited user (VIEWER role) and attempt an action that requires higher permissions (e.g., creating a user). Verify the permission error shows the translated message from errors.json FORBIDDEN key: 'You do not have permission to perform this action'.

steps.expected_result: Form populated with limited user credentials

steps.visual_check: Dashboard loaded for viewer user. Sidebar may show limited navigation based on permissions.

steps.expected_result: User list page loads (or permission denied)

steps.target: Create button (if visible) or attempt restricted action
steps.expected_result: Permission denied error displayed — the API returns FORBIDDEN with translated messageKey
steps.visual_check: Permission error visible: 'You do not have permission to perform this action' (from errors.json FORBIDDEN). If the Create button is hidden for viewers, navigate directly to /system/users/new to trigger the error. No raw key 'errors:FORBIDDEN' visible.

input_data.expected_text: You do not have permission to perform this action

steps.expected_result: Error text matches errors.json FORBIDDEN translation
Raw translation key 'errors:FORBIDDEN' is NOT visible — frontend resolved it via t()
